field.label: Customer

import.hint: The customer is the company or person who is paying for the shipment. Look for labels like "Bill To", "Bill Freight To", "Customer", "Billed To", or "Third Party". Extract only the company/person name — address details belong in other fields.

examples.inputText:
BILL FREIGHT TO:
ACME Logistics Inc
123 Main Street
Toronto, ON M5V 2T6

matchingCriteria.additionalInstructions: Consider email address and website domains if they are present

field.description: The customer for the order

field.description: cool stuff

field.description: Where this object came from

field.label: Custom money
field.description: test

field.label: Requested pickup

import.hint:
Always derive from the first stop whose type is "Pick"
If that stop shows an "appointment" indicator (APPT, APPOINTMENT, DROP APPT, DELIVERY APPT, PICKUP APPT, SCHD/SCHED, or Scheduled Time), treat it as a strict point-in-time:
- dateTimeInLocation === dateTimeInLocationEnd (same value). Both dateTimeInLocation and dateTimeInLocationEnd must be identical to that fixed‑appointment timestamp.

Otherwise build the window from that stop's own earliest and latest timestamps (e.g. PLAN → ETA).

field.description: A date range that the customer is requesting the load be picked up within

field.label: Requested delivery

import.hint:
Always derive from the last stop whose type is “Drop”
If that stop shows an “appointment” indicator (APPT, APPOINTMENT, DROP APPT, DELIVERY APPT, PICKUP APPT, SCHD/SCHED, or Scheduled Time), treat it as a strict point-in-time:
- dateTimeInLocation === dateTimeInLocationEnd (same value). Both dateTimeInLocation and dateTimeInLocationEnd must be identical to that fixed‑appointment timestamp.

Otherwise build the window from that stop's own earliest and latest timestamps (e.g. PLAN → ETA).

field.description: A date range that the customer is requesting the load be delivered within

field.label: Actual pickup date
field.description: The specific date and time when the load is picked up

field.label: Status
field.description: The status of the order

field.label: Connections
field.description: Records that are related to the order, for example emails, quotes, etc. that have been created or updated by the order.

field.label: Full ID
field.description: Human-readable identifier for manifest

field.label: Equipment
field.description: This represents the type of equipment the carrier must have

field.label: Invoices
field.description: Invoices connected to the order

field.label: PO number

import.hint: !!! IMPORTANT - CRITICAL FIELD - Look for labels: PO#, PO Number, Purchase Order, P.O., Customer PO, Shipper PO, PO Num. Often appears near receiver/consignee information. Extract the alphanumeric reference exactly as shown.

field.description: A purchase order number is typically provided by the receiver, and captures an order to for example restock inventory at a warehouse

field.label: Ref number

import.hint: !!! IMPORTANT -CRITICAL FIELD - Look for labels: Ref#, Reference, Reference Number, Order Ref, Customer Ref, Booking Number, Ref Num. This is the primary general-purpose reference identifier. Prefer this field if only one reference number is visible and the label is ambiguous.

field.description: The ref num is a general purpose field to store reference ids from other system. Prefer this over loadTenderNum if available

field.label: Current location
field.description: The current location of the order

field.label: Load tender number

import.hint: !!! IMPORTANT -CRITICAL FIELD - Look for labels: Load Tender#, Tender Number, BOL#, Bill of Lading, Shipper Ref, Carrier Ref, Load Number, Load Tender Num. Common on rate confirmations and BOL documents from shipper to carrier.

field.description: A reference number for a load provided by the shipper that a carrier will often use to reference a BOL or order
